commit.message:
fix: auto-format with ruff before committing in orchestrator (#68)

The CLI agent doesn't run our pre-commit hooks, so PR #67 failed CI on
ruff formatting.

Fix: `commit_and_push()` now runs `ruff format` and `ruff check --fix`
before `git add -A`. This ensures every agent commit passes formatting
checks regardless of CLI output.

Failures are suppressed — the worktree might contain non-Python files,
and formatting shouldn't block commits.

Co-authored-by: Colin Cheung <11246116+ColinCee@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Migrate workstation mode from X11/xmonad to Wayland/Hyprland (#16)

Replace the packages_gui array with the Wayland/Hyprland stack to
match the sneivandt/dotfiles desktop profile:

- Add: hyprland, hypridle, hyprlock, hyprpaper, uwsm, waybar, wofi,
  mako, grim, slurp, wl-clipboard, qt5-wayland, qt6-wayland,
  xorg-xwayland, gammastep, playerctl, otf-font-awesome
- Remove: xmonad, xmonad-contrib, xorg, xorg-server, xorg-xinit, picom,
  dunst, rofi, feh, redshift, flameshot, xclip, rxvt-unicode,
  urxvt-perls, xterm, code, ttf-sourcecodepro-nerd,
  noto-fonts-cjk, noto-fonts-emoji
- Simplify NVIDIA dialog to nvidia-open (Turing+, recommended),
  nvidia (pre-Turing), or skip (mesa/nouveau default)
- Remove xf86-video-vesa fallback (not applicable on Wayland)
- Remove xmonad pacman hook
- Update unit tests and README accordingly

commit.message:
Wire up SFMLRenderer in main.cpp for end-to-end Renderer abstraction

- Add isOpen(), close(), setMouseCursorVisible() to Renderer base class
- Add pollEvent(sf::Event&) to SFMLRenderer for SFML-specific event polling
- Fix resetView() to use current window size instead of initial default view
- Replace all direct sf::RenderWindow calls in main.cpp with renderer methods:
  window.clear/display -> renderer.clear/display
  window.setView(gameView) -> renderer.setView(cx, cy, viewW, viewH)
  window.setView(sf::View(sf::FloatRect(...))) -> renderer.resetView()
  window.isOpen/close/setMouseCursorVisible -> renderer equivalents
  window.pollEvent -> renderer.pollEvent
- Replace dash ghost sf::RectangleShape rendering with renderer.drawRect()
- Remove sf::View, sf::Color, sf::RectangleShape usage from main.cpp
- SaveSlotScreen still uses renderer.getWindow() for resolution changes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: enable parallel heuristic search for criterion=recon (#10)

* feat: enable parallel heuristic search for criterion=recon (criterion 5)

Three changes:
1. reconcile.c get_recon_score(): replace rand() with rand_r(&thread_seed)
   under #ifdef _OPENMP guards so each thread uses its own private PRNG state.
2. reconcile.c get_recon_score(): add temp_top = NULL after each
   duplicate_tree() result is consumed, fixing a double-free that occurred
   when hs_free_thread_state() tried to dismantle the stale pointer left by
   the local temp_top2 shadowing the global threadprivate temp_top.
3. treecompare2.c: remove the forced nthreads=1 fallback for criterion==5.
4. main.c: update nthreads help text to remove "not available for
   criterion=recon".

commit.message:
fix: ASCII description, snapshot cleanup in ENA test

Two bugs found during first live AWS run:

1. RegisterImage description contained em dash (U+2014) — AWS EC2
   rejects non-ASCII chars in description parameter. Replaced with
   ASCII hyphen.

2. EBS snapshot leaked after failures — snapshotId was scoped inside
   the try block so the finally cleanup block couldn't see it.
   Hoisted to outer scope + added deleteSnapshot() call in finally.
   Also adds deleteSnapshot() to src/aws/image.ts.

Manually deleted the leaked snapshot: snap-09e01477c1d0a514c

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: background knowledge base indexing with battery-aware rates

- New battery detection module (src/battery.rs): cross-platform battery status
- New knowledge indexing module (src/knowledge_index.rs): async background indexing
  * Configurable size limits (default 20MB, not surprising)
  * Battery-aware rate adaptation: fast on AC, imperceptibly slow on battery
  * Generates plaintext INDEX.md with category structure and keywords
  * Atomic writes prevent corruption, incremental updates possible
  * Partial coverage with unindexed category fallback guidance
- Config extended (src/config.rs): KnowledgeIndexSettings
  * knowledge_index_enabled: bool
  * knowledge_index_size_limit_gb: f64 (default 0.02 = 20MB)
  * knowledge_index_battery_delay_ms: u64 (default 100ms)
- Integration in main.rs: spawns indexing task on startup
- Steering text updated: documents knowledge index and search strategy
  * Models taught to check INDEX.md first
  * Fallback guidance for unindexed categories
- 50 unit tests passing, zero compilation errors
- 3 new modules added to lib.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use Rekordbox phrase analysis (PSSI) for cue labels when available

- Add read_phrases_anlz(): reads PSSI section from .EXT file via pyrekordbox
- Add _build_beat_ms_lookup(): maps absolute beat index → ms from PQTZ
- Add _PHRASE_LABELS: mood/kind → label table (High/Mid/Low phrase sets)
- Add _trim_phrases(): intelligent MAX_CUES trimming (Intro + Outro always
  kept, then fill by priority: Chorus > Up > Bridge > Down > Verse)
- process_track() now tries PSSI first; falls back to mel cosine analysis
  only when no PSSI data is present (audio file not required for PSSI path)

feat: add 5 production fixes and 4 competitor-matching features

Production Fixes:
- A1: CSP meta tag with SRI hashes on CDN scripts
- A2: Focus trapping in all modals with trapFocus/releaseFocus utility
- A3: Keyboard navigation (tabindex/role on collapsible headers, keydown handlers)
- A4: Lazy attachment streaming for per-recipient files (memory efficiency)
- A5: Event delegation for attachment lists, templates, and saved lists

Competitor Features:
- B1: Conditional content in templates (if/ifNot/ifEquals/ifNotEquals syntax)
- B2: Email validation before send with modal for issues
- B3: Suppression/blocklist management with localStorage persistence
- B4: Inline image insertion in rich text editor

Also adds i18n keys for all new UI strings across all 7 languages.

commit.message:
Fix scraper suite: allevents location parsing, dedup dedup, Dice slug bail, garbage OG filter

- allevents.js: fix JSON-LD Place location parsing (loc.address.streetAddress, not loc.streetAddress)
  add getBoroughByCoords() fallback using lat/lng bounding boxes
  fix URLs (remove trailing #, use correct category paths)
  add nightlife category
- allevents.js: fix image extraction (typeof string check)
- nycdata.js: give each event unique source_url with event_id query param to prevent global dedup collapse
- eventbrite.js: remove orphaned duplicate code block (lines 288-340)
- nycparks.js: replace dead RSS scraper with no-op (parks events covered by nycdata tvpp-9vvx)
- songkick.js: remove pages 2-3 (return HTTP 406 from non-browser IPs)
- timeout.js: update to working URLs; add isGarbageTitle filter to OG fallback

commit.message:
perf(maglev): extend range analysis to handle Generic* arithmetic ops

Range analysis induction variable detection and forward propagation
now recognize GenericIncrement, GenericDecrement, GenericAdd,
GenericSubtract, and GenericMultiply — not just CheckedSmi* variants.

After global promotion replaces LoadGlobal/StoreGlobal with Phi nodes,
loop counter increments become GenericIncrement(Phi). Previously these
were invisible to range analysis, so they kept overflow checks (JO deopt).
Now they are converted to Int32Increment when the computed range fits i32.

Similarly, accumulator patterns like sum += value are detected through
GenericAdd, enabling Int32Add lowering when total accumulation is bounded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace cubemap array shadows with 2D shadow atlas

Replace GL_TEXTURE_CUBE_MAP_ARRAY shadow maps with a single GL_TEXTURE_2D
shadow atlas. Each light gets a 3x2 tile block (6 cubemap faces) at an
index-based grid position, with per-face glViewport and per-light
scissored glClear.

This eliminates all glFramebufferTextureLayer calls during the shadow
pass. On macOS, each FBO attachment change triggered a Metal render pass
transition (endRenderPass -> submitCommandBuffer -> kernel roundtrip ->
beginRenderPass), which accounted for ~32% of total CPU time. The atlas
approach keeps the driver in a single render pass for the entire shadow
phase.

Key changes:
- r_shadow.c: Single FBO with 2D depth atlas, viewport/scissor per tile
- r_light.c: Populate per-light shadow atlas UV metadata in UBO
- light.glsl: Manual cubemap face selection + 2D PCF shadow sampling
- uniforms.glsl: sampler2DShadow replaces samplerCubeArrayShadow
- r_shadow_tile_size cvar (default 256) replaces r_shadow_cubemap_array_size
- Shadow caching preserved via stable index-based tile placement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: normalise spec constructors to consistent 2-ctor pattern (#93)

## Phase 6: Consistent specification constructor patterns

### Goal
Normalise all spec constructors to a consistent 2-constructor pattern:
one full-DI constructor (for tests) and one parameterless constructor
(for JSON deserialisation) that chains to it with defaults.

### Changes

#### Constructor cleanup
- **`LinkTaskSpecification`** — removed intermediate `(IFileManagement)`
constructor
- **`CopyTaskSpecification`** — removed intermediate `(IFileManagement)`
constructor
- **`CloneGitRepositoryTaskSpecification`** — collapsed 4 constructors
to 2; parameterless now chains to full-DI
- **`LinkExpandTaskSpecification`** — removed intermediate
`(IFileSystem)` constructor

#### New interfaces
- **`IGitRunnerFactory`** / **`GitRunnerFactory`** — replaces the
`this.gitRunner ?? new ProcessGitRunner(logger)` null-coalescing
anti-pattern. The factory creates `ProcessGitRunner` with the real
logger at execution time.
- **`IEnvironmentVariableWriter`** / **`EnvironmentVariableWriter`** —
wraps `Environment.SetEnvironmentVariable`, making
`EnvironmentVariableTaskSpecification` testable without real env-var
side effects

#### Factory wiring
- **`ShellTaskSpecification.ExecuteAsync`** — now uses the injected
`IShellInvokerFactory` instead of an inline `switch` expression
(completes Phase 3 wiring that wasn't applied during merge)

### Verification
- 0 warnings, 103 tests pass

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use eval7 for eq calculations (#48)

* Replace HandTriple equity with eval7 strength scoring (#47)

- Add eval7-based hand strength evaluation in solver/ranges.py
- build_strength_map(): precompute per-combo strength using eval7.evaluate()
  - River: single 7-card eval, Turn: avg over 46 runouts, Flop: avg over C(remaining,2)
- compute_equity_by_strength(): weighted fraction of villain range hero beats
- Add _combos_conflict() for hero-villain card exclusion (bug fix)
- Update filter_range_by_equity() to use strength_map instead of classify_cache
- Update ev_calculator.py to use new strength functions
- Remove old _classify_hand, build_classify_cache, build_equity_map, compute_hand_equity_vs_range
- Remove unused imports (HandTriple, hand_types classifiers, HoleCards)
- Add 18 new tests in test_strength.py (133 total, all passing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add __eq__ and __hash__ to Card for proper dict lookups

Card objects lacked equality/hash, causing strength_map lookups to fail
when different Card instances represented the same card. This made all
hands return 0.5 equity, resulting in every hand choosing max bet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add equity vs villain range to solver tooltip

- Compute equity vs full villain range per combo in solve_spot()
- Average combo equities to hand-key level
- Add equity field to SolverComboResult and SolverHandResult
- Show equity percentage in hand grid tooltip on hover

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix combo tuple ordering mismatch in strength_map lookups

ALL_COMBOS creates tuples as (lower_rank, higher_rank) but
_hand_key_to_combos creates (higher_rank, lower_rank). Added
_normalize_combo() to ensure consistent dictionary keys regardless
of which code path created the Card objects.

Previously, ~50% of villain combos were missing from strength_map
lookups, causing most hands to show 0% equity.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Show per-combo equity breakdown in hand grid tooltip

Tooltip now shows each combo's equity on a second line:
AQs: Bet 75% (EV +3.2) | Equity 27.6%
AhQh: 25.3%  AdQd: 28.1%  AsQs: 30.2%  AcQc: 26.8%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add per-runout exact equity calculation (#47)

Replace averaging-based equity with per-runout exact comparison using
numpy vectorization. Tooltip equity now matches Flopzilla methodology:
- AA on 432 monotone: 77.2% (was 93.2%, Flopzilla shows 77.15%)
- Suit-sensitive: AA with spade = 86.3%, without = 68.1%
- Performance: ~2s for 300 hero combos vs 600 villain range on flop

Strength-map averaging kept for filter_range_by_equity sorting (fast
approximation is sufficient for range bucketing).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: start epmd before net_kernel in split_dc_SUITE; ci: epmd -daemon

split_dc_SUITE requires Erlang distribution (peer module). On GitHub
Actions (and any fresh environment) epmd is not pre-started, causing
net_kernel:start to fail with nodistribution.

Fix: call os:cmd("epmd -daemon") before net_kernel:start in
init_per_suite. The call is idempotent — safe when epmd is already
running.

Also start epmd explicitly in the CI 'ct' step as a belt-and-suspenders
measure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(maglev): zero register file in emit_direct_call_0 to prevent SIGSEGV

Add rep stosq register file zeroing to both the mono-cache HIT and
cache MISS paths in emit_direct_call_0.  The other direct-call emitters
(emit_direct_call_1, emit_direct_call_2) already zeroed theirs, but
emit_direct_call_0 skipped it under the assumption that JIT callees
always initialise every slot before reading.  In the closure_counter_1k
benchmark, stale stack memory in the uninitialized register file caused
the callee to dereference a garbage context pointer before the prologue
could store RSI, triggering a SIGSEGV on Linux.

HIT path: RAX/RCX/RDI are dead after the BA save/push sequence, so the
zeroing needs no register save/restore.

MISS path: R10 (ctx_ptr) and R11 (entry point) are live, so they are
push/popped around the rep stosq, matching the pattern in
emit_direct_call_1 and emit_direct_call_2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
